summary: Replayed the shared NGINX edge publication from merged `main`, forcing service-specific edge converges to load the canonical platform topology before rebuilding the shared certificate SAN set, then re-verified `wiki.example.com` and `draw.example.com` publication end to end.

verification.observed: `uv run --with pytest python -m pytest -q tests/test_edge_publication_playbooks.py tests/test_outline_playbook.py tests/test_n8n_playbook.py tests/test_dozzle_playbook.py` passed with `6 passed in 0.16s` from merged `main`.
`HETZNER_DNS_API_TOKEN=... make configure-edge-publication env=production` completed successfully from merged `main` commit `6f2e8ab6` with `nginx-lv3 ok=62 changed=5 failed=0 skipped=13`.
`curl -I https://wiki.example.com` and `curl -I https://wiki.example.com/_health` both returned `HTTP/2 200`, and the live certificate SANs include `DNS:draw.example.com` and `DNS:wiki.example.com`.
`grep -n 'wiki\.lv3\.org\|draw\.lv3\.org' /etc/nginx/sites-available/lv3-edge.conf` on `nginx-lv3` returned the expected `server_name` entries for both hostnames.

`python3 scripts/sync_docs_to_outline.py verify --repo-root /Users/live/Documents/GITHUB_PROJECTS/platform_server --base-url https://wiki.example.com --api-token-file /Users/live/Documents/GITHUB_PROJECTS/platform_server/.local/outline/api-token.txt` returned `outline living collections verified`.

notes: This mainline receipt supersedes the branch-local `2026-03-28-adr-0021-shared-edge-certificate-repair-live-apply` receipt as the canonical public-edge evidence for this repair.
The live replay also preserved the newly published `tasks.example.com` hostname in the shared SAN set, confirming the repair coexists with the newer public-edge topology present on `main`.
The `main` worktree already had `build/changelog-portal/` and `build/docs-portal/` present before replay, so the shared edge could republish both generated static portals during the repair.
